# 11 USC 109 -- Section 109(g) Bankruptcy Filing Bar [2026 Guide]
> Dismissed bankruptcy? Section 109(g) blocks refiling for 180 days. Learn both triggers, exceptions, and exactly when you can file again. Free 2026 guide.

## About
109g.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- The 180-Day Rule in Bankruptcy -- When You Cannot Refile -- 109(g): https://109g.org/180-day-rule.html
- 180-Day Filing Bar Calculator -- Section 109(g): https://109g.org/calculator.html
- Bankruptcy Dismissed for Cause -- Can I Refile? -- 109(g): https://109g.org/dismissed-for-cause.html
- What You Can Do During the 180-Day Filing Bar -- 109(g): https://109g.org/how-to-get-around-it.html
- 11 USC 109 -- Section 109(g) Bankruptcy Filing Bar: https://109g.org/
- Serial Filer Protections -- How 109(g) and 362(c) Work Together: https://109g.org/serial-filer-protection.html
- Voluntary Dismissal and the 109(g)(1) Filing Bar: https://109g.org/voluntary-dismissal-bar.html
- 109(g)(2) -- Voluntary Dismissal After a Relief from Stay Motion: https://109g.org/voluntary-dismissal.html
- 109(g) vs Other Filing Bars -- Filing Bars, Discharge Bars, and Stay Limits: https://109g.org/vs-other-bars.html
- 109(g)(1) -- Willful Failure to Obey Court Orders: https://109g.org/willful-failure.html
- Section 109(g) FAQ -- Bankruptcy Filing Bars Explained: https://109g.org/frequently-asked-questions.html
